title: Commissioner of Income Tax v. Reliance Industries Ltd.
summary: Clarified the treatment of depreciation under Section 32 of the Income Tax Act for 'block of assets'. Held that the written down value (WDV) of the entire block must be calculated and depreciation applies to the block as a whole, not individual assets. Where WDV becomes nil or negative, no depreciation is allowable.
key_holdings: Depreciation under Section 32 applies to 'block of assets' as a whole
Written Down Value (WDV) calculated for the entire block
When WDV becomes zero or negative, no depreciation is admissible
Short-term capital gain/loss on sale of asset calculated by reference to the block
Slump sale provisions and depreciation interact differently from individual asset sales
why_important: Definitive ruling on depreciation calculations for businesses. Directly impacts income tax computation for all businesses with fixed assets.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Directly relevant to tax calculations for businesses — depreciation claims affect taxable income.
